title: Add and use libpod/Container.Terminal() helper
body:
This just gets ctr.config.Spec.Process.Terminal with some null checks, allowing several places that open-coded this to use the helper.

In particular, this helps the code in
pkg/domain/infra/abi/terminal.StartAttachCtr(), that used to do: `ctr.Spec().Process.Terminal`, which looks fine, but actually causes a deep json copy in the `ctr.Spec()` call that takes over 3 msec.

Signed-off-by: Alexander Larsson <alexl@redhat.com>

comments.body:
@alexlarsson: Adding the "do-not-merge/release-note-label-needed" label because no release-note block was detected, please follow our [release note process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md) to remove it.

If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>
@alexlarsson in order to pass CI you need to add the magic `[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]` string to the commit message along with an explanation why the change doesn't need tests.

LGTM.

Any instance of ctr.Spec() is *massively* expensive and there are only a very few cases where it's warranted. Finding and removing them seems like a very good idea.

Is Container.Config() equally expensive? It looks like it?
I'll have a look at this.
I ran some profiling last year and tackled the low-hanging fruits. `Config()` was a big one.
